data.title: Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) Earnings Growth And 35.9% Margin Reinforce Bullish Narratives
data.description:
Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) has just wrapped up Q4 2025 with total revenue of US$102.7 million and basic EPS of US$1.25, setting the stage for its Q1 2026 reporting season with a trailing twelve month EPS of US$4.77. Over the last six reported quarters, total revenue has moved from US$85.5 million in Q3 2024 to US$102.7 million in Q4 2025, while basic EPS has shifted from US$1.00 to US$1.25. This gives investors a clearer line of sight on how earnings power is tracking into the new year. With a trailing net profit margin of 35.9% and a share price of US$70.44, the story now pivots to how durable those margins look against the latest commentary and expectations.

See our full analysis for Stock Yards Bancorp.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to set them against the most common narratives around Stock Yards Bancorp to see which views the latest results support and which they call into question.

Advertisement

Net interest margin steady around 3.5%

  • Across the last four quarters, net interest margin sat in a tight band between 3.46% and 3.56%, with the latest trailing twelve month figure at 3.53%.
  • For those who view SYBT as a steady regional franchise, it is notable that this stable margin sits alongside consistent loan balances in the US$6.5 billion to US$7.0 billion range. This suggests the bank has kept pricing and volume relatively balanced, without the swings that more aggressive lenders sometimes experience.

Cost to income ratio just above 53%

  • The cost to income ratio over the last year is 53.42%, compared with quarterly readings of 54.5%, 53.87% and 52.99% across 2025, indicating that operating expenses took a little over half of income across the period.
  • Those who worry that regional banks could face rising regulatory or operating costs may see a mixed picture here. The ratio stays in a fairly tight 52.99% to 56.18% range, while trailing net profit margin is 35.9%. This shows that expenses are meaningful but have not prevented SYBT from converting a substantial share of revenue into profit.

Asset quality helped by lower non performing loans

  • Non performing loans on a trailing basis are reported at US$13.0 million, versus quarterly figures that range from US$16.1 million to US$22.2 million in the earlier periods provided.
  • Critics who focus on regional bank credit risk will likely pay attention to how this level compares with the US$7.0 billion loan book. Even though headlines around the sector often stress loan quality concerns, the supplied data here points to non performing balances that are a small fraction of total loans, which contrasts with bearish narratives that treat all regional banks as facing similar levels of credit strain.

data.title: Has Newsmax (NMAX) Fallen Too Far After A 70% One Year Share Price Slump
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Newsmax at around US$6.71 is a bargain or a value trap, the starting point is understanding what that share price actually builds in.
  • The stock has seen mixed recent returns, with a 5.5% gain over the last 30 days, a 14.8% year to date decline and a steep 70.3% drop over the past year.
  • These moves have kept attention on how investors are reassessing the company, with media coverage focusing on Newsmax's positioning in the U.S. news market and its efforts to grow audience share across cable and digital platforms. That backdrop helps frame the debate about whether recent weakness reflects sentiment, fundamentals, or both.
  • Right now, Newsmax holds a value score of 2 out of 6. The sections that follow will compare different valuation approaches and then finish with a broader way to think about what this score really means for long term investors.

Newsmax scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Newsmax Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value.

For Newsmax, the latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $106.2 million. The model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, which starts with this current position and then applies cash flow projections over the coming years.

The projections used include free cash flow of $19.3 million in 2027 and extend out to 2035, reaching $90.4 million by that year. Only the near term estimate is from an analyst, with the later years extrapolated to build a longer term picture. All figures are in US$.

After discounting these projected cash flows and adding a terminal value, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of US$12.86 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about US$6.71, this framework suggests the stock is 47.8% undervalued on these assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Newsmax is undervalued by 47.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 54 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Newsmax.

Approach 2: Newsmax Price vs Sales

For companies where revenue is a key focus, the P/S ratio is often a useful yardstick because it compares what investors are paying for each dollar of sales rather than profit, which can be volatile or negative.

What counts as a “normal” P/S ratio usually reflects how quickly revenue is expected to grow and how risky those cash flows look. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty tend to pull it down.

Newsmax currently trades on a P/S ratio of 4.58x. That sits above the Media industry average of 1.16x and also above a peer group average of 0.23x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Newsmax is 1.03x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/S might be based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

The Fair Ratio can be more helpful than a simple comparison with peers or the industry because it adjusts for those company specific drivers rather than assuming all media stocks should trade on the same multiple. Set against this Fair Ratio, Newsmax’s current 4.58x P/S suggests the shares are pricing in a materially richer valuation.

Result: OVERVALUED

Here, you give Newsmax a story that ties together your assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins into a forecast. You can then compare your resulting fair value with the current price to see whether the stock looks expensive or cheap to you, and watch that view update automatically when new information such as earnings or news is added. data.title: Dynatrace To Acquire Bindplane To Deepen Control Over Telemetry Data
data.description:
• Dynatrace (NYSE:DT) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Bindplane.
  • The deal brings Bindplane’s open standards based telemetry pipeline technology into the Dynatrace platform.
  • The acquisition is intended to expand telemetry data management, optimization, and compliance capabilities for Dynatrace customers.

For investors watching observability and application performance tools, Dynatrace sits at the intersection of monitoring, security, and AI driven analytics. As software environments grow more complex and data heavy, enterprises are paying closer attention to how telemetry is collected, processed, and stored across clouds and on premises systems.

The planned integration of Bindplane’s technology signals a focus on giving customers more control over what data they ingest, how it is routed, and what it costs. Readers may want to watch how Dynatrace executes on this integration and how customers respond to expanded options around data control and regulatory compliance.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Dynatrace by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Dynatrace.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 3 things going right for Dynatrace that every investor should see.

The Bindplane deal points directly at one of the big pressure points in observability, which is the sheer volume and cost of telemetry flowing from cloud and on premises systems. By adding an open standards based pipeline that can sit closer to where data is created, Dynatrace is aiming to let customers filter, shape, and secure data before it hits the core platform. For you as an investor, that sits next to the company’s AI powered Dynatrace Intelligence launch, because both depend on having clean, well managed data feeding the models. It may also speak to customer concerns around compliance and data residency, which are increasingly important as enterprises scale multi cloud observability.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Dynatrace Narrative

  • The acquisition lines up with the narrative that a unified, AI powered observability platform can deepen customer integration by increasing control over telemetry, which may support higher multi product adoption over time.
  • Integrating an additional telemetry stack could add execution risk, especially as Dynatrace already faces competition from Datadog, New Relic, and cloud providers that also lean on open standards.
  • The narrative already highlights AI observability and consumption based pricing, but does not fully address how owning an open standards pipeline might affect Dynatrace’s relationship with open source tools such as OpenTelemetry.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Dynatrace to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Folding Bindplane into the platform could be complex, and any delay or disruption may distract from execution on sales, AI features, or pricing changes that are already underway.
  • ⚠️ Competitors such as Datadog, New Relic, and the major clouds also support open standards, so customers may still view telemetry pipelines as interchangeable, which could limit differentiation.
  • 🎁 If Dynatrace can use Bindplane to reduce customers’ ingestion costs and improve data quality, it may strengthen the case for consolidating more workloads on its platform.
  • 🎁 Better control over data routing, masking, and encryption could make Dynatrace more relevant in regulated industries where compliance and data governance are central buying criteria.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how quickly Dynatrace brings Bindplane features into its core platform, how pricing is packaged around telemetry optimization, and what early customer feedback looks like. Pay attention to any commentary on data cost savings, compliance use cases, and whether this opens doors to larger or more complex deals, especially relative to offerings from Datadog and the cloud hyperscalers. The degree to which Dynatrace ties Bindplane into its AI roadmap will also help show whether this acquisition becomes central to the long term product story or stays more tactical.

data.title: Constellation Energy Reshapes Clean Power Role With Calpine Deal And Crane Test
data.description:
• Constellation Energy has agreed to acquire Calpine in a $26.6b deal, expanding its generation portfolio to around 55 GW and a claimed 10% of U.S. clean energy production.
  • The company is seeking to restart the Crane nuclear plant to support a 20 year clean power supply agreement with Microsoft.
  • Regulatory concerns have emerged, with the PJM Interconnection market monitor questioning the pace and structure of the Crane restart plan.

Constellation Energy, NasdaqGS:CEG, is moving through a period of significant change, with the Calpine acquisition reshaping the business into a much larger and more diversified clean power producer. The company’s current share price stands at $292.77, with a 1 year return of 34.9% and a 3 year gain of about 4x, even as the stock has seen a 20.1% decline year to date. For investors, these mixed share price moves sit alongside a major shift in scale and customer exposure.

The Crane nuclear restart tied to the long term Microsoft agreement adds another layer to consider, because it connects Constellation directly to the growing data center power demand theme while putting regulatory scrutiny in sharper focus. How the company responds to PJM’s concerns and integrates Calpine’s assets could influence its earnings drivers, exposure to power market pricing, and its role in supplying large corporate buyers seeking long duration clean energy contracts.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Constellation Energy by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Constellation Energy.

3 things going right for Constellation Energy that this headline doesn't cover.

The Calpine deal and Crane restart plan together push Constellation further into the center of clean power supply for large corporates, especially data centers. Adding around 55 GW of capacity and a claimed 10% share of U.S. clean energy production gives the company more scale to compete with peers such as NextEra Energy, Vistra and Duke Energy for long-term, contract-based revenue. The 20 year Microsoft agreement effectively pre-sells a portion of future output to a single, creditworthy customer, which can support planning for fuel, maintenance and potential financing. At the same time, the PJM market monitor’s objection to the proposed transfer of Capacity Interconnection Rights highlights how dependent this plan is on regulator acceptance of both timing and structure. For you as an investor, the key tension is clear: larger, contracted exposure to AI-related power demand on one side, and more visible regulatory and execution risk on the other, especially as Constellation integrates Calpine while working through the Crane approvals in a public way.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Constellation Energy Narrative

  • The Calpine acquisition and Crane restart directly align with the narrative focus on long-term, higher-margin contracts with large customers and expanded nuclear-heavy capacity tied to data center demand.
  • PJM’s concerns around the Crane interconnection and capacity rights could challenge assumptions in the narrative about how smoothly nuclear restarts and market access progress.
  • The specific structure of the Microsoft deal and the scale of the Calpine platform are not fully reflected in the existing narrative, which may understate customer concentration and integration complexity.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Constellation Energy to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Regulatory pushback on the Crane restart could delay capacity additions and affect the timing of deliveries under the Microsoft contract.
  • ⚠️ Larger exposure to a few long-duration data center agreements increases customer concentration risk if contract terms or usage patterns change.
  • 🎁 The combined 55 GW portfolio and claimed 10% share of U.S. clean energy output position Constellation to compete for additional long-term corporate and data center deals.
  • 🎁 The Crane restart and Calpine integration support the existing narrative of using nuclear and gas capacity to serve growing AI-driven electricity demand with long-duration contracts.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how regulators rule on Constellation’s requested waivers for Crane, whether any conditions are attached, and how quickly the plant restart schedule evolves. Progress updates on integrating Calpine’s fleet, including any disclosed cost or revenue synergies, will also matter for the earnings mix. On the commercial side, keep an eye on new long-term agreements with data center operators or large corporates, and whether terms look similar to or different from the Microsoft deal. Together, these signposts can help you judge whether Constellation is converting its expanded scale and nuclear footprint into durable, contracted cash flows while keeping regulatory and concentration risks under control.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Dorchester Minerals (DMLP) After Strong Multi‑Year Returns?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether Dorchester Minerals at around US$28.70 still offers value, or if most of the opportunity is already priced in.
  • The stock has returned 1.7% over the past week, 3.6% over the past month, 23.4% year to date, 9.0% over 1 year, 28.6% over 3 years and 221.8% over 5 years. This naturally raises questions about what you are paying for today.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Dorchester Minerals as part of broader discussions around income focused energy partnerships and their place in diversified portfolios. That context helps explain why some investors are reassessing both the income profile and the risks tied to commodity exposed assets like this one.
  • The current valuation score for Dorchester Minerals sits at 2 out of 6. This reflects that it screens as undervalued on only part of our checklist. The next step is a closer look at the standard valuation methods used and, finally, a framework that can help you read those valuation signals with more confidence.

Dorchester Minerals scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Dorchester Minerals Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to the present.

For Dorchester Minerals, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, starting from last twelve month free cash flow of about $132.5 million. Simply Wall St then extends analyst style estimates to build a ten year path of projected cash flows, which reach about $171.4 million in 2035, all in $ and then discounted using the chosen rate each year.

Adding up these discounted cash flows and the terminal value results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $74. That compares to a recent share price of roughly $28.70, implying an intrinsic discount of about 61.2% based on this DCF model.

In plain terms, this model suggests investors are currently paying a material discount to the cash flows implied by these projections.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Dorchester Minerals is undervalued by 61.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 54 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Dorchester Minerals.

Approach 2: Dorchester Minerals Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. It lets you compare how the market prices one business against others that generate profits in the same currency and sector.

What counts as a “normal” P/E often reflects what investors expect for future earnings and how much risk they see in those earnings. Higher expected growth or perceived lower risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E.

Dorchester Minerals currently trades on a P/E of about 25.1x, compared with an Oil and Gas industry average of about 14.9x and a peer group average of about 18.4x. Simply Wall St also uses a proprietary “Fair Ratio”, which is the P/E level suggested for Dorchester Minerals after weighing factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can give a more tailored reference point than a simple industry or peer comparison because it adjusts for the company’s own characteristics. Compared with this Fair Ratio, Dorchester Minerals’ current P/E suggests the shares are trading at a premium.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so this is where Narratives come in, giving you a clear story behind your assumed fair value and your expectations for Dorchester Minerals’ future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative is simply your view of what the business is, how it might develop, and how that story flows into a financial forecast, then into a fair value estimate that you can compare with today’s price.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives are available on the Community page and are designed to be straightforward, so you can quickly connect your thesis about Dorchester Minerals to specific numbers without needing complex models.

These Narratives help you decide whether to hold, add or reduce exposure by lining up your fair value against the current market price. They refresh automatically when new information comes in, such as earnings releases or major news.

data.title: Aerie Realmakers Community Puts Authenticity At Center Of AEO Growth Story
data.description:
• Aerie, a brand under American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO), has launched the Aerie Realmakers Community, a new creator program centered on authentic content.
  • The program offers creators commissions and campaign opportunities while prohibiting retouched photos and AI generated imagery.
  • This launch builds on Aerie's marketing focus on authenticity and community driven engagement with its customer base.

American Eagle Outfitters, trading at around $18.19, has seen mixed share performance, with a 7 day return of a 5.1% decline and a 30 day return of 11.5%. Over longer periods, the stock has delivered a 67.2% return over 1 year and 47.4% over 3 years, while the 5 year return of a 40.7% decline points to a more uneven long term picture.

For investors watching NYSE:AEO, the Aerie Realmakers Community highlights how the company is leaning into consumer demand for authenticity and community based marketing. The impact of this program can be assessed over time through customer engagement trends, brand perception and how effectively it supports Aerie within the broader American Eagle portfolio.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for American Eagle Outfitters by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on American Eagle Outfitters.

📰 Beyond the headline: 3 risks and 3 things going right for American Eagle Outfitters that every investor should see.

Aerie’s Realmakers Community leans into a real-content approach that sets it apart from peers such as Victoria’s Secret’s Pink, Abercrombie’s Gilly Hicks and L Brands’ intimates offerings. By banning retouched photos and AI generated imagery, Aerie is tying its marketing engine directly to values that already resonate with its target customer. For American Eagle Outfitters, this keeps Aerie’s growth story closely linked to brand trust and word of mouth rather than just paid media. The commission structure and campaign access give creators a financial reason to stay active with the brand, which can help keep customer acquisition costs in check if the program scales well. Given recent share-price volatility and mixed short term returns, investors watching AEO can view this as another test of how effectively the company can convert brand equity and community engagement into consistent sales across both online and stores.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The American Eagle Outfitters Narrative

  • The Realmakers Community supports the existing focus on expanding Aerie and OFFLINE by deepening customer engagement and potentially reinforcing demand for broader product assortments.
  • If creator driven campaigns fail to translate into traffic or ticket growth, the marketing spend behind Aerie could challenge the narrative that higher brand investment will support improved margins over time.
  • The explicit stance against AI generated imagery and retouching adds a brand perception angle that is not fully reflected in the current narrative, which is more focused on store optimization and financial discipline.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for American Eagle Outfitters to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Creator programs can increase marketing complexity and costs if Aerie needs to offer higher commissions or frequent campaigns to keep participants engaged.
  • ⚠️ A focus on unretouched, non AI content may limit the volume and flexibility of marketing materials if creators struggle to meet quality expectations under these rules.
  • 🎁 A strong authenticity message can help Aerie stand out in a crowded intimates and activewear market, potentially supporting pricing power and customer loyalty.
  • 🎁 If the Realmakers Community drives consistent user generated content, AEO may benefit from a scalable source of brand promotion that supports the broader multi brand portfolio.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors should watch for any commentary from management on how many creators join the Realmakers Community, how often their content is used in campaigns and whether Aerie traffic, conversion or engagement metrics move meaningfully after launch. It is also worth tracking how this authenticity focused positioning compares with responses from competitors and whether AEO keeps allocating more marketing dollars to Aerie relative to the core American Eagle banner.

data.title: Assessing New Found Gold (TSXV:NFG) Valuation After Queensway Dropkick Drilling Extends High Grade Zone
data.description:
Advertisement

Event overview and why it matters

New Found Gold (TSXV:NFG) recently reported final 2025 drilling results from the Dropkick Zone at its Queensway Gold Project, confirming an extended high grade gold domain that remains open along strike and at depth.

With drilling scheduled to resume in late Q2 2026, the focus now shifts to how continued work at Dropkick and along the Appleton Fault Zone could influence project scale, funding needs, and investor expectations for this exploration name.

See our latest analysis for New Found Gold.

New Found Gold’s share price has moved to CA$2.90, with a 16% 1 month share price return contrasting with a 38.17% 3 month share price decline and a 12 month total shareholder return of 87.10%. Recent momentum looks mixed as the market digests Dropkick results alongside April’s CAD 100 million follow on equity offering and new CAD 105 million credit facility.

If this kind of gold exploration story has your attention, it could be a good time to see what other producers look like across the sector by reviewing our 29 elite gold producer stocks

With the stock at CA$2.90 and trading well below the CA$5.00 analyst price target, along with fresh equity and credit now in place, is the recent pullback creating an entry point or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Preferred Price-to-Book of 2.4x: Is it justified?

On a P/B of 2.4x at a CA$2.90 share price, New Found Gold trades well below both its peer average of 11.5x and the wider Canadian Metals and Mining industry at 3.3x.

P/B compares a company’s market value to its book value. For early stage explorers this can be a useful shorthand for how the market prices each dollar of assets on the balance sheet. For New Found Gold, a 2.4x multiple suggests the market is assigning a lower value to its asset base than peers, even though this is primarily an exploration story with limited revenue and current losses.

Against the Canadian Metals and Mining industry average P/B of 3.3x and a peer group closer to 11.5x, New Found Gold’s current multiple looks restrained. That kind of gap often reflects the company’s lack of meaningful revenue at CA$6m, ongoing losses of CA$47.57m, share dilution over the past year and the early stage nature of the Queensway and Kingsway projects, rather than any clear premium for future cash flows.

data.title: Valley National Bancorp (VLY) Margins Strengthen And Back Bullish Efficiency Narratives Ahead Of Q1 2026
data.description:
Advertisement

Valley National Bancorp Q1 2026 earnings snapshot

Valley National Bancorp (VLY) opened 2026 with Q1 results that build on its recent momentum, coming off Q4 2025 total revenue of US$505.9 million and basic EPS of US$0.34, supported by trailing 12 month net income of US$569.0 million and basic EPS of US$1.02. The bank has seen total revenue move from US$365.9 million in Q4 2024 to US$505.9 million in Q4 2025, while quarterly basic EPS stepped up from US$0.20 to US$0.34 over the same period. This sets up Q1 2026 against a backdrop of expanding profitability. With trailing net profit margins and dividends both in focus, investors are likely to read the latest earnings through the lens of how durable these margin levels appear.

See our full analysis for Valley National Bancorp.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to line them up against the most common narratives around Valley National Bancorp to see which stories fit the data and which ones start to look stretched.

See what the community is saying about Valley National Bancorp

Margins and costs point to efficiency gains

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Valley National Bancorp reported a net profit margin of 30.9%, with a cost to income ratio of 54.44% and net interest margin of 3.05%, compared with a 2.85% net interest margin and 57.98% cost to income ratio for the trailing 12 months a year earlier.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative expects digitalization and operational efficiency to support margins. The recent move from a 2.85% to 3.05% net interest margin and from a 57.98% to 54.44% cost to income ratio provides concrete data for that view, although it also sets a higher bar for maintaining these levels over time.
    • Supporters of the bullish angle on efficiency can point to trailing 12 month net income of US$569.0 million on US$1.8b of revenue as evidence that the current margin structure is already delivering stronger profitability than in the previous year.
    • At the same time, the consensus narrative flags rising compliance and technology costs as an ongoing risk, so any reversal in the cost to income ratio from the current 54.44% level would be important for investors tracking whether the efficiency story is playing out as expected.

Strong recent margin and earnings trends are exactly what bullish investors reference when they talk about Valley's profit story. The dedicated bull case sets out how those themes could play out over time for the business 🐂 Valley National Bancorp Bull Case.

Credit quality: non performing loans creeping higher

  • Non performing loans on a trailing 12 month basis sit at US$433.9 million compared with US$359.5 million a year earlier and US$296.3 million in the period ending Q3 2024, against a loan book that is around US$50.1b.
  • Bears focus on credit risk, especially commercial real estate, and the steady move in non performing loans from US$296.3 million to US$433.9 million gives them specific numbers to point to when questioning how resilient recent earnings and margins might be.
    • Critics highlight that ongoing exposure to commercial real estate and region specific shocks could require higher provisions, and the rise in non performing loans over the last few reported periods is consistent with that concern.
    • On the other hand, the consensus narrative notes that specialized verticals such as healthcare and fund finance have shown low credit losses, so investors may want to separate pockets of stress from areas where asset quality has been described as relatively stable.

For readers who want to see how the cautious credit view stacks up against the numbers sector by sector, the detailed bear case breaks that argument out in full 🐻 Valley National Bancorp Bear Case.

Valuation tension: higher P/E and DCF gap

  • With a current share price of US$13.77, Valley National Bancorp trades on a trailing P/E of 13.4x versus peer and US Banks industry averages of 12.8x and 11.7x respectively, while a DCF fair value of about US$27.50 and an analyst price target of US$14.96 both sit above the current market level.
  • Consensus narrative supporters point to the mix of earnings growth, a 3.2% dividend yield, and the DCF fair value gap as reasons the current price could be out of step with fundamentals, while the higher than peer P/E keeps the discussion grounded in what investors are actually paying today.
    • The trailing 12 month basic EPS of US$1.02 is what underpins both the 13.4x P/E and the DCF fair value of US$27.50, so any shift in earnings would directly affect both sides of that valuation tension.
    • Meanwhile, the analyst price target of US$14.96 is close to the current US$13.77 price, which fits with the idea that, despite a higher P/E than peers, the stock is viewed as roughly fairly valued when forecasts and risks are taken into account.
